text.title: Stakeholder Analysis — Agentic Workflow and Gotchas
text.summary: How to drive stakeholder analysis with subagents: definition-freezing protocol, classifier prompt patterns, and LLM failure modes to guard against.

rule.statement: Before classifying any stakeholder, persist a glossary.md and model-choice.md in the program BA folder declaring: stakeholder definition (verbatim from BABOK 3 §3.2 or ISO 21502 §4.4), responsibility model chosen, salience model chosen, and rationale for rejecting alternatives.
rule.rationale: LLMs treat "stakeholder" as a synonym for "user" or "persona" by default; injecting the frozen definition verbatim into the system prompt is the only reliable mitigation.

rule.statement: Sponsor signs the register baseline; subsequent diffs require sponsor acknowledgment only for attitude or accountability changes, not for contact info updates.
rule.rationale: Keeps sign-off lightweight while protecting the high-stakes fields that affect engagement strategy.

list.item: definitions-linter-agent (sonnet) — validates the register against the chosen model; RACI: exactly one A per row; RASCI: at most one A, multiple S allowed; flags violations as PR comments
salience-classifier-agent (sonnet) — applies Mitchell-Agle-Wood to each stakeholder; emits one of 8 types with citations per axis
babok-mapper-agent (sonnet) — maps the register to BABOK 3 §3.2 deliverables for audit traceability
password-scrubber-agent — sweeps stakeholder docs for leaked credentials or PII before commit

example.note: Two-stage prompt: freeze definitions first, then classify against them.
example.code:
STAGE 1 — Definitions agent: Given the program charter, choose: - responsibility_model: one of [RACI, RASCI, DACI, RAPID] - salience_model: one of [Mendelow, Mitchell-Agle-Wood, both] - stakeholder_definition: verbatim from BABOK 3 §3.2 OR ISO 21502 §4.4
Emit STRICT JSON: { "responsibility_model": "...", "salience_model": "...", "stakeholder_definition": "...", "rationale": "<=3 sentences", "rejected_alternatives": ["..."] } Rule: cite the source clause; no invention.
STAGE 2 — Salience classifier: For each stakeholder in the register, apply Mitchell-Agle-Wood: - power: ability to impose will (H/M/L + evidence) - legitimacy: socially accepted claim (H/M/L + evidence) - urgency: time-criticality of claim (H/M/L + evidence, cite deadline/SLA) Emit type: dormant|discretionary|demanding|dominant|dangerous|dependent|definitive|non-stakeholder. Rule: any axis without evidence => "unknown"; do not infer urgency from tone.

antipattern.reason: Default LLM training conflates these; fix by forcing the chosen stakeholder_definition into the system prompt verbatim every call.

antipattern.description: Agent over-assigns R and C in RACI — every role gets something.
antipattern.reason: Constrain output: max 1 A, max 3 R, require one-line rationale per C; validate with the lint script.

antipattern.description: Agent infers urgency from message tone rather than from a cited deadline or SLA.
antipattern.reason: Tone-based urgency is statistically biased; require "urgency_source: deadline/SLA/contractual" and reject entries without it.

antipattern.description: BABOK section citations hallucinate plausible-sounding clause numbers.
antipattern.reason: Maintain a curated babok-toc.md and constrain the agent to cite only from it.
Names, attitudes, and salience scores are confidential; one accidental share ends stakeholder trust.

list.item: Freezing definitions and choosing the responsibility model
